title: NOC 73400 – Heavy equipment operators | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73400 professionals are the literal "earth-movers" of the Canadian economy. As a TEER 3 occupation, Heavy Equipment Operators operate massive machinery used in the construction and maintenance of roads, bridges, airports, oil pipelines, and buildings. In 2026, as Canada accelerates its "National Infrastructure Plan" and ramps up critical mineral mining for the EV battery supply chain, these operators are classified as Strategic Development Talent. For immigration, this role is a top-tier candidate for the OINP In-Demand Skills Stream and the 2026 Trade-Specific Express Entry draws.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group734 – Operators, utility maintenance and related workersMinor Group7340 – Operators, utility maintenance and related workersNOC Code73400

duties: Earth Moving: Operate heavy equipment such as backhoes, bulldozers, loaders, and excavators to move, load, and grade earth, rock, and gravel.
Clearing & Roadwork: Use bulldozers or other heavy machinery to clear brush and stumps for logging or to build access roads at mining sites.
Structural Support: Operate equipment equipped with pile driver heads to drive piling into the earth for building and bridge foundations.
Waterway Deepening: Operate heavy dredging equipment to deepen waterways or reclaim earth fill for coastal projects.
Surfacing & Paving: Operate heavy paving and surfacing equipment to spread, level, and compact concrete or asphalt during highway construction.
Mining Operations: Operate massive power shovels to excavate rock and ore from open-pit mines, quarries, or strip mines.
Material Handling: Operate specialized heavy equipment to move, load, and unload cargo in industrial settings or cargo-handling yards.
Pre-Operational Checks: Conduct mandatory daily pre-trip inspections of all mechanical components and fluid levels to ensure safety.
Maintenance: Perform basic routine maintenance such as cleaning, lubricating, and refilling fuel/fluids on various machinery types.
Precision Control: Utilize onboard computer systems and GPS-guided grade controllers to execute excavations within millimeter tolerances.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is typically required.
Training: Completion of a one-to-two-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of college courses and intensive on-the-job training.

Certification:Compulsory in Quebec (as Heavy Equipment Operator).Voluntary in ON, AB, BC, SK, and NL.Specific voluntary trades in Ontario include: Dozer (636C), Excavator (636B), and Tractor Loader Backhoe (636A).
Compulsory in Quebec (as Heavy Equipment Operator).
Voluntary in ON, AB, BC, SK, and NL.
Specific voluntary trades in Ontario include: Dozer (636C), Excavator (636B), and Tractor Loader Backhoe (636A).
Red Seal Endorsement: Available to qualified operators upon successful completion of the interprovincial Red Seal exam; highly recommended for interprovincial mobility.
Licensing: A valid driver’s license is required, and a clean driving record is usually a prerequisite for employment.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, specialized Mining Shovel Operators or those on major infrastructure projects in Northern Canada often earn annual salaries between $95,000 and $135,000 inclusive of overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the shift toward modular construction and "Green Mining" for lithium and copper will require operators to be more tech-literate. While automation is being integrated into some site preparation tasks, the high-skill requirements for complex excavations and grading ensure that human operators will remain indispensable through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for heavy equipment operators in 2026 is Stable to Strong, with a particularly "Good" outlook in high-growth regions like Ontario, British Columbia, and Saskatchewan. In Ontario alone, approximately 26,150 people are employed in this NOC, with massive infrastructure investments in transit (Ontario Line), power generation, and highways (Highway 413) driving demand. According to March 2026 Job Bank data, while the national outlook is "Moderate," the "Replacement Demand" is acute, as nearly 40% of the workforce is over the age of 50.
Advanced Machine Technology: Work with the latest GPS-guided grading systems, semi-autonomous excavators, and telematics-equipped bulldozers.
Diverse Industrial Exposure: Transition easily between residential construction, heavy civil engineering, forestry, and open-pit mining operations.
Superior Union Benefits: Many operators belong to the International Union of Operating Engineers (IUOE), which provides industry-leading wages and comprehensive health/pension plans.
Strategic PR Pathways: This NOC was explicitly highlighted in 2026 OINP updates as a high-priority "In-Demand" skill, offering a streamlined path to Permanent Residency.
pathways: Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): This NOC is a high priority for 2026 category-based selection draws with lower CRS requirements.
Ontario (OINP) In-Demand Skills Stream: Explicitly targets NOC 73400 for those with 6–9 months of experience in Ontario.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A reliable route for operators with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
Skilled Trades BC: A streamlined pathway for certified operators to gain a provincial nomination in British Columbia.
